mission: Freeze the current GP principal-system provider path strategy as a split between mock-now executable truth, official watch-only lanes, and gated actual-provider-later routes without letting supplier folklore bypass the canonical booking proof model.

decisions.title: Treat IM1 Pairing as the only current principal-system actual-later path.
decisions.rationale: Current official guidance still points patient-to-GP-practice booking at IM1 Pairing Patient APIs, and the current public supplier roster names only Optum (EMISWeb) and TPP (SystmOne).

decisions.title: Keep GP Connect Appointment Management as watch only.
decisions.rationale: The lane is officially live, but new supplier development is currently paused and the current use cases remain bounded to existing consumer estates and staff flows.

decisions.title: Keep BaRS visible but not baseline for principal GP-system booking.
decisions.rationale: BaRS is relevant interoperability infrastructure, but current official guidance places principal-system patient booking against IM1 Pairing and places BaRS in broader referral or urgent-care contexts.

decisions.title: Local adapter simulation is mandatory now.
decisions.rationale: The booking provider truth seam is a top-priority mock-now family and the blueprint explicitly requires deterministic simulation before supplier claims are trusted.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4E. Commit path, revalidation, booking record, and compensation

decisions.title: Manual practice handoff is fallback only, not success.
decisions.rationale: Manual practice contact preserves continuity but must still flow through ExternalConfirmationGate and the current BookingConfirmationTruthProjection before any calm booked state appears.

decisions.title: Proof objects remain named and separate.
decisions.rationale: Seq_036 refuses generic yes/no capability claims. Search, hold, authoritative confirmation, manage support, and reminder readiness stay distinct dimensions tied to named canonical objects.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4B. Provider capability matrix and adapter seam

decisions.title: Actual provider work remains fail-closed.
decisions.rationale: The current gate set still blocks real provider work until booking MVP, sponsor posture, diagrams, approver, environment, mutation flag, and watch-register clearance all exist.

live_gates.title: Provider path evidence is published
live_gates.summary: Seq_036 publishes the path matrix, proof dossier, decision register, and gap register.

live_gates.title: Approved provider scorecards exist
live_gates.summary: Seq_022 already froze the GP/IM1/booking supplier scorecard family and due-diligence bars.

live_gates.title: Architecture and data-flow diagrams are current
live_gates.summary: The architecture baseline exists, but the live-provider-specific diagram digest still needs named refresh before any real path can open.

live_gates.title: Credible booking MVP is approved
live_gates.summary: Actual provider work stays blocked until a bounded booking MVP is explicitly named and approved.

live_gates.title: Sponsor and commissioning posture is explicit
live_gates.summary: A real provider path cannot open without named sponsor ownership and commissioning posture.

live_gates.title: Named approver and environment are present
live_gates.summary: Actual-later remains fail-closed until a named approver and target environment are declared.

live_gates.title: Mutation flag is enabled explicitly
live_gates.summary: Real provider mutation stays blocked unless ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true is supplied deliberately.

live_gates.title: No unresolved blocker remains in the watch register
live_gates.summary: The gap register still contains unresolved supplier, governance, and evidence blockers.

live_gates.title: Phase 0 external foundation gate is open
live_gates.summary: Seq_020 still reports Phase 0 entry withheld, so actual provider motion cannot be treated as current-baseline execution.

gap_digest.summary: Optum (EMISWeb) is publicly listed for IM1 Pairing, but exact booking, hold, and manage semantics still need supplier PIP evidence.
TPP (SystmOne) is publicly listed for IM1 Pairing, but exact booking, hold, and manage semantics still need supplier PIP evidence.
GP Connect Appointment Management new supplier development is currently paused, so it cannot be treated as the current Vecells expansion lane.
Current GP Connect Appointment Management value is estate-specific and oriented to existing consumer suppliers and staff flows.
BaRS is broader referral interoperability and should not be mistaken for the baseline patient-to-GP-practice principal-system booking path.
A real provider path cannot open without named sponsor ownership and commissioning posture.
Actual-later remains fail-closed until a named approver and target environment are declared.
The architecture baseline exists, but the live-provider-specific diagram digest still needs named refresh before any real path can open.
Real provider mutation stays blocked unless ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true is supplied deliberately.
The gap register still contains unresolved supplier, governance, and evidence blockers.

scorecard_digest.focus: ProviderCapabilityMatrix, supplier and integration-mode variance, slot search snapshots, revalidation, async confirmation truth, and stale capability drift

integration_priority_digest.minimum_mock_fidelity: Simulate slot search snapshots, capability resolution, held versus non-exclusive reservation truth, revalidation failure, confirmation_pending, disputed callbacks, same-commit read-after-write proof, manage freeze, waitlist fallback, and wrong-patient freeze. Queue or webhook acceptance may not imply booked truth.
truth_proof_digest: Current supplier binding and authoritative read or outcome observation path accepted for the same supplier, organisation, action scope, and transport assurance profile.
BookingConfirmationTruthProjection(confirmationTruthState = confirmed) backed by a durable provider reference or same-commit read-after-write proof for the same BookingTransaction.

im1_pairing: There are no current plans to decommission IM1.
gp_connect_supplier_progress: Appointment Management is only relevant for existing suppliers.
referrals_and_bookings: The NHS App and third party software options use

official_guidance.title: IM1 Pairing integration
official_guidance.summary: NHS England still positions IM1 Pairing as the current principal-system route. The page names Optum (EMISWeb) and TPP (SystmOne), says there are no current plans to decommission IM1 Pairing, and shows the path from prerequisites through SCAL, supported test, assurance, and live rollout.
grounding: Optum (EMISWeb) and TPP (SystmOne) are the currently named provider suppliers.
IM1 Pairing has no current decommission plan.
Supplier-specific functionality differs and detailed technical packs arrive later through PIP acceptance.
The route remains long-lead and assurance-heavy rather than instantly usable.

official_guidance.title: GP Connect Appointment Management
official_guidance.summary: The GP Connect Appointment Management page describes the API as live and highlights its primary use in integrated urgent care, care navigation, and care coordination. It also names current embedded consumer suppliers instead of inviting new baseline product onboarding.
grounding: The page describes care navigation, care coordination, and integrated urgent care use cases.
Approved consumer suppliers are listed in current embedded estates rather than described as an open baseline expansion lane.
The examples are staff and service-coordination oriented, not Vecells patient-shell self-service truth.

official_guidance.title: GP Connect supplier progress
official_guidance.summary: The supplier-progress page explicitly says new supplier development for Appointment Management is currently paused while existing consumer suppliers remain listed.
grounding: Appointment Management new supplier development is currently paused.
Consumer estates are current but bounded to listed suppliers and use cases.

official_guidance.title: Guidance for referrals and bookings specific use cases
official_guidance.summary: NHS England's guidance explicitly says patient-to-GP-practice booking applications are generally integrated with principal clinical systems through IM1 Pairing Patient APIs, while BaRS is presented in referral and urgent-care use cases.
grounding: Patient-to-GP-practice booking is generally integrated through IM1 Pairing Patient APIs.
BaRS appears alongside urgent care and inter-service referrals rather than as a principal GP-system route.
The guidance helps separate principal-system booking from broader referral interoperability.

official_guidance.title: NHS Booking and Referral Standard
official_guidance.summary: The standard defines BaRS as a FHIR-based interoperability standard between patient record systems for booking and referral information exchange. That is important, but it still does not make BaRS the current Vecells baseline for principal-GP-system patient self-service booking.
grounding: BaRS is a standard for interoperability between patient record systems.
The standard is broader than one GP principal system estate and needs bounded interpretation inside Vecells.
